ocr.text: In assessing the impact of dietary interventions on pig growth performance, the data presented in Table 1 highlights key metrics
associated with average daily gain (ADG) during the initial week post-weaning. Specifically, the ADG interval for days 1 to 7 is
crucial for understanding early growth trajectories that can influence overall productivity.
Observation day  Lawsonia intracellularis (log10 bacteria/g faeces)  Porcine circo virus type 2 (log10copies/g faeces)
(ADG interval)  DM%  No pigs  ADG (sd)b  Positive (%)c  Mediand  Positive (%)c  Mediand
The table delineates the average daily gain (ADG) alongside the dry matter percentage (DM%) of the feed, which is pivotal for

evaluating feed efficiency during this critical growth phase. 

Notably, the data indicates that groups with a DM% of less than
11.3 demonstrated a significant average daily gain, with a mean ADG of 0.043 kg per pig per day. Furthermore, the positive
response rate, quantified as Positive (%)c, reached 100% in this cohort, underscoring the efficacy of the dietary regimen
employed. The median values presented in the table further elucidate the consistency of growth performance across the sampled
populations. Collectively, these findings provide valuable insights into the effectiveness of nutritional strategies designed to
optimize early growth in pigs, thereby informing future research and practical applications in swine production.
